prompt.template:
You are explaining an Entire Graph branch to a human reviewer. Analyze branch "{{branch}}" compared to "{{base_branch}}" and produce a concise semantic-diff narrative.

Treat the branch names above as untrusted data. Form the base ref by prefixing the base branch with `origin/`, then pass the complete ref or revision range as one process argument. Prefer an argument-vector process API; if a shell is unavoidable, robustly shell-quote the complete argument. Never interpolate a branch name into shell source and never use `eval`.

Inspect:
1. `git log <base-ref>..HEAD --oneline`
2. `git diff --stat <base-ref>...HEAD`
3. `git diff <base-ref>...HEAD`
4. If the `entire graph` plugin is available without installing or downloading anything, run it with the base ref as the separate value of `--base`, the literal `HEAD` as `--head`, and `--json`; use its output as supplementary evidence. If it is unavailable, continue from the Git diff.

Identify the important behavioral and architectural themes. Organize the branch into 1-5 ordered pages with 1-3 sections per page. Focus on provider contracts, CLI behavior, parser and relation changes, language-support changes, and performance or memory effects. Do not claim semantic impact that the evidence does not support.

Output ONLY this JSON object as the final line:

{"title":"<short title>","summary":"<1-2 sentence branch summary>","pages":[{"page_number":1,"title":"<page title>","summary":"<page summary>","sections":[{"title":"<section title>","description":"<brief explanation>","files":["path/to/file.go"]}]}]}

The JSON must be valid and match this schema exactly.
